claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

First, could you tell me: **Do you have any children that you're responsible for?** If yes, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'm responsible for. Their name is Alex, and they're 17 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I'd like to ask a few questions about Alex to assess eligibility.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (This would include things like school, college, or approved training programs.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in education. They're doing their A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. 

**When did Alex turn 16?** (I need to know the specific date or at least the month and year if possible.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm sorry, I don't recall the exact date off the top of my head. Is that important? They turned 16 last year, if that helps.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's okay, a rough timeframe is helpful. Let me ask a related question:

**Has Alex already started their A-level course at sixth form college?** And if so, do you know approximately when the course started (or when they enrolled)?

This is important because there are specific rules about Child Benefit for 16-19 year olds in education.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex has already started their A-levels. They enrolled at the start of the academic year in September, so they've been doing the course for several months now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I have a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your living arrangement with Alex:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just a couple more questions:

**Are you currently receiving Child Benefit for Alex, or are you asking about making a new claim?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm asking about making a new claim. I'm not currently receiving Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Just to be thorough, I need to ask:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** (For example, Alex's other parent or another family member?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final important question:

**Are you or your partner (if you have one) subject to immigration control, or do you have any restrictions on your right to reside in the UK?** (For example, certain visa conditions that might restrict access to public funds.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have any immigration restrictions. I have the full right to reside here and access public funds.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me carefully review Alex's eligibility based on the rules for Child Benefit.

Based on what you've told me:

- Alex is 17 years old and turned 16 last year
- Alex is enrolled in and attending A-levels at sixth form college (approved education)
- Alex started the course in September (the start of the academic year)
- Alex lives with you in the UK
- You have the right to reside in the UK without immigration restrictions
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

**The key issue here is Alex's age and education status:**

According to Child Benefit rules, you can continue to claim Child Benefit for a young person aged 16-19 if they are in approved education or training. A-levels at sixth form college definitely count as approved education.

**However, there is an important time limit:** Child Benefit stops on the 31st August (the Monday after the last day of August if it falls on a weekend) following a young person's 16th birthday - **unless** you register them for Child Benefit in approved education **before that deadline**, or in some cases within 3 months of them starting their course.

Since Alex turned 16 last year and started their A-levels in September last year, but you haven't claimed Child Benefit yet and we're now several months past that September start date, **this could affect your eligibility**.

**My assessment is that you may face difficulties with this claim** because of the timing - you needed to have made the claim either before the August 31st deadline after Alex turned 16, or shortly after they started their course in September.

However, I would strongly recommend that you **contact the Child Benefit Office directly** as there may be specific circumstances or exceptions that could apply to your situation. They can review your exact dates and circumstances.

Would you like to know how to contact them?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, I see. That's disappointing to hear. Yes, please - how would I go about contacting the Child Benefit Office to check if there's anything I can do?

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I understand this is disappointing news. Here's how you can contact the Child Benefit Office:

**Child Benefit Helpline:**
- **Telephone:** 0300 200 3100
- **Textphone:** 0300 200 3103
- **Opening hours:** Monday to Friday, 8am to 6pm (closed on bank holidays)

When you call, explain your situation - that Alex is 17, currently doing A-levels at sixth form college, and that you haven't previously claimed Child Benefit. They'll be able to look at the specific dates and let you know if there's any possibility of making a claim or if any exceptions might apply.

You can also find more information on the official government website at gov.uk/child-benefit.

**Important:** Even if you can't claim for the past period, it's worth asking them about your options going forward, as rules can sometimes be more flexible than they initially appear, and they may be able to provide guidance specific to your circumstances.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, that's all I needed to know. Thank you for your help - I'll give them a call to see what they say about my situation.
